animal.draw-value.help: the number of cards you must <0>take at the end of your turn</0>.
animal.hand.player: This Animal card is in {player}'s hand.
animal.hand.you: This Animal card is in your hand.
animal.pile.player: This Animal card is in {player}'s pile.
animal.pile.player.empty: {player}'s Animal card pile is empty. Its placement value is considered to be 1.
animal.pile.player.hidden: This card is the <0>visible Animal card</0> at the top of {player}'s pile, face down. Its placement value is considered to be 1.
animal.pile.top.player: This card is the <0>visible Animal card</0> at the top of {player}'s pile.
animal.pile.top.you: This card is the <0>visible Animal card</0> at the top of your pile.
animal.pile.you: This Animal card is in your pile.
animal.pile.you.empty: Your pile of Animal cards is empty. Your placement value is considered to be 1.
animal.pile.you.hidden: This card is the <0>visible Animal card</0> at the top of your pile, face-down. Your placement value is then considered to be 1.
animal.place-value.help: the number of cards you must <0>place on your personal pile during your next turn</0>.
draw-pile.count: There {number, plural, one{is # card} other{are # cards}} left in the draw pile.
header.bear.player: Polar bear: {player} gives a Penalty card to another player
header.bear.you: Polar bear: give a Penalty card to another player
header.discard.player: {player} must discard {number, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}} from their hand
header.discard.you: Discard {number, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}} from your hand
header.draw.penalty.you: Draw {number, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}} from the River or your Penalties
header.draw.pile-penalty.you: Draw {number, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}} from the River, your Penalties or the draw pile
header.draw.pile.you: Draw {number, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}} from the River or the draw pile
header.draw.player: {player} must draw {number, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}}
header.draw.you: Draw {number, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}} from the River
header.fox.under-animal: Fox: the card can be placed face-down under your visible Animal
header.fox.under-pile: Fox: the card can be placed under your pile
header.moose.draw.more.player: Moose: {player} can draw up to {number} cards
header.moose.draw.one.player: Moose: {player} can draw 1 card or pass
header.moose.draw.two: Moose: Draw up to 2 cards or <pass>pass</pass>
header.moose.draw.two.player: Moose: {player} can draw up to 2 cards or pass
header.moose.fox.play: Place a card, use the power of the fox, or <pass>pass</pass>
header.moose.play.more: Moose: Place up to {number} cards to your pile
header.moose.play.more.player: Moose: {player} can place up to {number} cards to their pile
header.moose.play.one: Moose: Place 1 card to your pile or <pass>pass</pass>
header.moose.play.one.player: Moose: {player} can place 1 card to their pile or pass
header.moose.play.two: Moose: Place up to 2 cards to your pile or <pass>pass</pass>
header.moose.play.two.player: Moose: {player} can place up to 2 cards to their pile or pass
header.move-tokens.player: {player} moves the {animal1} or {animal2} token onto the Landscape
header.move-tokens.you: Move the {animal1} or {animal2} token onto the Landscape
header.play-card.player: {player} places {number, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}} from their hand to their pile
header.play-card.you: Place {number, plural, one{# card} other{# cards}} from your hand to your pile
header.puffin.draw.player: Puffin: {player} can take back their visible Animal card in hand
header.puffin.draw.you: Puffin: take your visible Animal card back into your Hand or <0>ignore</0>
header.puffin.trade.player: Puffin: {player} can swap a card from their Hand
header.puffin.trade.you: Puffin: swap a card from your Hand or <0>ignore</0>
header.token.left: The {animal} token moves to the left
header.token.right: The {animal} token moves to the right
header.walrus.player: Walrus: {player} can move the {animal} token one extra square
header.walrus.you: Walrus: move the {animal} token one square or <0>ignore</0>.
paw-print-token.help:
<underline>End of game</underline>
When the common draw pile is empty and the River can no longer be refilled, create a final draw pile using the cards in the Reserve that were put aside underneath the Reserve token during setup. Finish the current round as normal until you reach the player with the Paw print token. That player does not carry out their turn.
Then, each player carries out one final turn, which consists of playing through steps A and B but doesn't include drawing cards (i.e., ignore step C).
After this, the game is over and it's time for final scoring.
penalty-area.help: You receive penalties when you don't have enough cards in your hand to place on your pile, or when you have more than 7 cards in your hand after drawing.
penalty-area.player: There {number, plural, one{is # card} other{are # cards}} in {player}'s Penalty zone.
penalty-area.you: There {number, plural, one{is # card} other{are # cards}} in your Penalty zone.
power-card.help: To retrieve and benefit from this Power card, you must place the corresponding animal at the top of your pile.
power-card.you: This card is in your possession.
power.10: Choose a card from your Penalty zone and place it in another player's Penalty zone.
power.11: When drawing cards, you may choose cards from your Penalty zone as well as from the River.
power.20: When placing cards, you may place the final card underneath your personal pile.
power.21:
When placing cards, you may place the final card face down underneath your visible Animal card.
During final scoring, it counts as an animal type of your choice.
power.30: Increase or decrease your draw value by 1.
power.31: Increase or decrease your placement value by 1.
power.40: Immediately refill the River after each card you take.
power.41: When drawing cards, you may take 1 or more face-down cards from the top of the common draw pile.
power.50: Before placing your cards, swap a card from your Hand with a card from the River.
power.51:
Before placing your cards, take your visible Animal card back into your hand. Careful, because you now have a new placement value this turn.
If you don't have a visible Animal card, your placement value is considered to be 1.
power.60: Move the token corresponding to the main animal 1 extra card in the direction of your choice.
power.61: Move the token corresponding to the companion animal 1 extra card in the direction of your choice.
power.fox.under-last: Place face down under your visible Animal
scoring.help1: 1. For each animal, calculate the longest sequence of consecutive cards in your personal pile. Don’t count the cards that aren't part of the longest sequence, or any isolated cards.
scoring.help2:
Depending on the number of cards in each longest sequence, score points
for these animals according to the table below:
scoring.help3: Number of cards in the sequence
scoring.help5: 2. Depending on the number of sequences you determined in the previous step (which corresponds to the number of different animals), score points according to the same table.
scoring.help6: Example: If you determined 4 sequences, you score 6 additional points.
scoring.help7: Important: a sequence consists of at least 2 identical, consecutive Animal cards.
scoring.help8: 3. Then, reveal your Animal Totem tile and score points according to the Landscape card its corresponding token is on (0, 1, 3, 6, 10, or 15).
scoring.help9: 4. Deduct 1 point from your score for each card in your Penalty zone.
stock.count: This reserve of {number} cards will be used to form the final draw pile at the end of the game, so that each player plays the same number of turns.
totem.score: At the end of the game, each player reveals their Animal Totem and scores the points indicated by the Landscape card on which their token is placed (0, 1, 3, 6, 10 or 15).
tuto.animal.tiles: You'll need to move the Orca or Moose token on the Landscape. To do this, you need to move one token in one direction. The other will automatically go the other way.
tuto.animals: On the card you've just placed, these two icons indicate the main animal (in this case, the Orca) and the companion animal (in this case, the Moose).
tuto.animals.totem:
To choose, here's your Totem Animal. Other players can't see it.
At the end of the game, you earn as many points as the location of the Moose on the Landscape.
tuto.choice: On your turn, you must <0>choose one of these cards</0> and place it on your Pile, in front of you.
tuto.deposit-value:
On the card at the top of your pile, named the <0>visible Animal card</0>, this icon indicates your <0>Placement value</0>. On this turn, you can therefore place 2 cards.
<1>On your first turn, you had no visible Animal card, so your Placement value was 1.</1>
tuto.draw-phase: After moving the 2 Animal tokens, you must draw new cards.
tuto.draw-value:
On the card you've placed, this icon indicates your <0>Draw value</0>.
At the end of your turn, you draw the number of cards indicated here.
tuto.draw.cards: Now draw 3 cards of your choice from the River of cards.
tuto.end.trigger: The game goes on this way until the draw pile is depleted. The round is then completed so that everyone has played the same number of turns.
tuto.go: Now it's your turn to play. Good luck!
tuto.goal: Your goal is to make sequences of identical animals in this Pile. For each animal, you'll score points for <0>the longest sequence</0> of that animal. It's also interesting to <0>diversify your sequences</0> (more on this later).
tuto.hand: Here is your hand of <0>Animal cards</0>.
tuto.hand.limit: Beware: if you have <0>more than 7 cards in your hand</0> at the end of this phase, you must discard the excess into your <0>Penalty zone</0>. Each card in this zone will cost you 1 point at the end of the game!
tuto.moose: Move the Moose to the right.
tuto.moose.draw-more: The power of the Moose allows you to draw one card more or less in this step.
tuto.move-animal: Now that your Placement step is over, you must move the 2 Animal tokens shown on your visible Animal card: the Moose and the Fox.
tuto.move-animal.exception: If one of your animals is at the edge of the landscape, it will be moved towards the center. The second animal will then be moved automatically in the other direction.
tuto.move-animal.one: Move one of the 2 tokens in one direction, and the other will automatically move in the other direction.
tuto.opponent:
Your turn is over. Now it's your opponent's turn to do the same:
<0>A.</0> Add Animal cards to their pile and take a Power card.
<0>B.</0> Move Animal tokens.
<0>C.</0> Draw cards.
tuto.penalty: Similarly, if you have to place <0>more cards than you have in hand</0> during the Placement step, you receive <0>one penalty card for each missing card</0>.
tuto.place: Place this card on your Pile.
tuto.placed: This card has no effect: only the last card placed becomes your visible Animal card and affects the rest of your turn.
tuto.power: You immediately receive the Power card corresponding to the animal visible on this card, the Orca.
tuto.power.moose: You immediately take the Moose Power card.
tuto.power.orca:
The Orca's Power allows you to fill the River with cards as you draw from it.
Each power can be used as long as it is present in your play area and as long as no other player has picked it up.
tuto.powers:
These are the Power cards, each power is different, and each Animal has 2 possible powers (determined at random at the start of the game).
Click on the Power cards to discover their effect!
tuto.river: Cards are drawn from the River of cards: choose 4 cards from it and take them in your hand.
tuto.scoring:
Click on this tile or <1>here</1> to see the final scoring:
1. points for each Animal sequence
2. points for sequences diversity
3. points for your Totem Animal
4. penalty points
